Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 26 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 誰もいないと この事務所、しずかだな。
English: Sure is quiet here with nobody around.

Japanese: そうだ‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: 早く、真宵ちゃんを 出してあげないと‥‥。
English: I have to get Maya out of that detention cell soon!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's favorite plant. Its name is "Charley."

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんも 世話に慣れてきたようで、
English: Maya's gotten the knack of watering it lately.

Japanese: チャーリーくん、元気を 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: Charley's been perking up these days.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ボーイが、引き出しのドライバーの 角度をなおしているのが見える。
English: I can see the bellboy, getting the angle of that screwdriver in the drawer just right.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていた デスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: お客がもっと増えれば 座る機会もあるだろうが、
English: If we had more clients,
I would probably sit here more often.

Japanese: 今はソファでテレビを 見ていることの方が多い。
English: Lately, I've been spending more time on the couch, watching TV.

Japanese: 一度、真宵ちゃんがトノサマンの ポスターを持ってきて、
English: Maya brought in a poster of the Steel Samurai the other day.

Japanese: 張る、張らないで大ゲンカに なってしまった。
English: We had a big fight over whether to put it up or not.

Japanese: まだあきらめきれないらしく、 スキをうかがっているみたい。
English: I know she's just waiting for a chance to sneak it up on the wall.

Japanese: いやあ。やっぱりいいねえ、 シャバの空気は。
English: Ahh! Fresh air! I gotta say, freedom feels great.

Japanese: 明日の法廷、もう ムチャしないでくれよ。
English: Behave yourself in the courtroom tomorrow, okay?

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。 それは、どうかな？
English: Heh heh heh. Misbehaving's much more fun.

Japanese: もう御剣も、保釈金 払ってくれないぞ、たぶん。
English: It's not going to be so much fun when Edgeworth refuses to pay your bail again.

Japanese: うん。ムチャしない。
English: Right. I'll behave.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (Oh dear...)

Japanese: どうしようか？ これから‥‥。
English: Well, what should we do?

Japanese: あたし、留置所にいたから 何も知らないんだよ。
English: I don't know! I've been in detention this whole time.

Japanese: なるほどくんにまかせた！
English: I think I'll let you decide what we should do. Deal?

Japanese: どう？ ‥‥何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Any thoughts you want to share?

Japanese: とにかくあたし、留置所に いたからねー。
English: Well, I was in detention all day.

Japanese: ちょっと、考える時間が ほしいなあ。
English: I think I'd like more time to think.

Japanese: （留置所では、きっと千尋さんの ことでも考えていたんだろうな）
English: (Poor Maya, she probably thought about Mia the whole time she was in there...)

Japanese: あたしに何か見せるより、 早く調査に行こうよ！
English: Don't waste time showing me things! We have to get cracking on this case!

Japanese: テキは、またベツの証人を つれてくるんだから！
English: You know the enemy has more tricks up his sleeve! He's bringing in another witness!

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: あっ！　なるほどくん！
English: Hey! Nick, it's you!

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶだった みたいだね‥‥御剣検事。
English: I'm glad Mr. Edgeworth made it through the day okay.

Japanese: よかった‥‥。
English: It's a relief...
Hey.

Japanese: なんで、あんなこと したんだよ！
English: Why'd you do that, anyway?

Japanese: わからない。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: でも‥‥あたし、何か しなくちゃ、って思って。
English: I... just knew I had to do something.

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃあ、あたしは お姉ちゃんじゃないけど‥‥。
English: I know I'm not the lawyer my sister was...

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ。
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: まあ、おかげで 助かったんだけどさ‥‥。
English: Well, you did save the trial...

Japanese: もう、ムチャしないでくれよ。
English: Just, behave from now on, okay?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: O... okay.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch since I came in. A real pro.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: もう、取り調べとか受けたの？
English: Have you been questioned yet?

Japanese: ううん。‥‥まだ。
English: No... not yet.

Japanese: さっき、イトノコさんが 来たんだよ。
English: Detective Gumshoe was here just now.

Japanese: “初犯だし、取り調べがすんだら 出られるッス”だって。
English: He said "seeing as this is your first offense, we'll let you go after questioning."

Japanese: ‥‥よかった。
English: Whew...

Japanese: あ、それと、“保釈金を用意しとく ッス”って言ってたんだけど。
English: Oh, and he wanted me to get bail money ready.

Japanese: なるほどくん、払っといてね。
English: You can pay for me, okay?

Japanese: やれやれ。‥‥いくら？
English: Huh? How much?

Japanese: わかんない。あとで 請求書が来るんじゃないかな。
English: I don't know. I guess they'll send you a bill or something.

Japanese: （“保釈金”‥‥か。なぜか、 高そうなイメージが‥‥）
English: (Why do I picture giant bales of money every time I hear the word "bail"?)

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんは、どう‥‥？
English: Any luck with Mia...?

Japanese: そ‥‥それが‥‥ 全然、通じないの。
English: None... I can't get through to her at all.

Japanese: 必死でがんばったのに‥‥。
English: I tried... I really did!

Japanese: もう、どうしていいか わからない。
English: I don't know what to do...

Japanese: やっぱり、修行を休んでたのが マズかったのかなあ。
English: I think I probably shouldn't have stopped my training.

Japanese: （こっちはこっちで、 シンコクだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... she sounds like she really did do her best.)

Japanese: （今度、近所に滝がないか、 調べてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should check and see if there are any waterfalls in the local area...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ もう会えないのかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if I'll ever see my sister again...

Japanese: なるほどくんは、いいね。
English: It must be nice, Nick.

Japanese: それがあれば、自分が “弁護士だ”って言えるもん。
English: You can just show that to anyone and say "I'm a lawyer!"

Japanese: その点、霊媒師はねえ‥‥。
English: But, spirit mediums, well...

Japanese: そのカッコをしてれば、 わかる人には、わかるかも。
English: I dunno, with your clothes, I bet at least some people would recognize you...

Japanese: ‥‥でもこれ、修行中の霊媒師の 装束だから。
English: But these are medium-in -training clothes...

Japanese: あたし‥‥ １人前に、なれるかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if I'll ever be a proper medium...

Japanese: あ‥‥それ、ナツミさんが言ってた カクダイ写真だね。
English: Isn't that the enlarged photo Lotta was talking about?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Uh huh.

Japanese: うーん。
English: Huh...

Japanese: 大きいのはいいけど、やっぱり よく見えないねー。
English: It's nice and big... but you can't really see the faces any clearer, can you?

Japanese: あたしの‥‥おかあさん。
English: My... mother.

Japanese: （写真を見せたはいいけど、 かけるコトバが見つからない）
English: (Now that I've shown her the picture, I'm not so sure it was the right thing to do.)

Japanese: （‥‥見せるんじゃなかったな）
English: (No... I'm pretty sure it was the wrong thing to do.)

Japanese: あたしも見たいよ！　ヒョッシー。
English: I want to see Gourdy, too!

Japanese: いれば、の話だろ。
English: If he really exists.

Japanese: だって、こうして写真が あるわけでしょ。
English: Well, I'd say a photograph is pretty good proof!

Japanese: この事件が終わったら、 ツリザオ持って、湖に行こうね！
English: Once this trial is over, let's get some fishing poles and go down to the lake!

Japanese: （湖に行くのはいいけど、 “ツリザオ”ってなんだ‥‥？）
English: (Even if there is a monster in that lake, fishing poles sound like a BAD idea...)

Japanese: ‥‥この弾丸の線条痕が、凶器の ピストルと一致したんだよね。
English: So the markings on this bullet and the barrel of the pistol matched?

Japanese: お。よくおぼえてたな。
English: Hey, I'm surprised you remembered that!

Japanese: カルマ検事に、 さんざんイヤミ言われたからね。
English: How could I forget after von Karma rubbed it in so much?

Japanese: で？　どれがセンジョーコンなの？
English: So, which one of these are the ballistic markings?

Japanese: ‥‥実は、ぼくもわからない。
English: I'm... not really sure.

Japanese: そのピストル、御剣検事の指紋が ついてるんだよね。
English: Mr. Edgeworth's prints are on this pistol, aren't they?

Japanese: そう。右手の指紋がね。
English: Yes... the prints from his right hand.

Japanese: でも‥‥、撃ったときについた わけじゃ、ないみたいなんだ。
English: But they must have gotten on there at some time other than the shooting.

Japanese: え！　そうなの？
English: What? Really!?

Japanese: （犯人は、左手で撃っている からな‥‥）
English: (The shooter used his left hand, after all...)

Japanese: ごめんね。 ‥‥特に思い当たること、ないな。
English: Sorry... I'm fresh out of ideas.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あっ！　なるほどくん！ やっと来てくれたよ‥‥。
English: Hey, Nick! You finally came!

Japanese: さっき、手続きが終わったから、 もう帰っていいって。
English: They just finished the paperwork, I'm free to go.

Japanese: おつとめ、ごくろうさま。
English: Free at last, eh?

Japanese: 聞いて！　聞いて！ 取り調べの人、ヒドいんだよ！
English: Those interrogators were really mean!

Japanese: “今度は何やったの？” だって。
English: They were like "okay, what did you do THIS time?"

Japanese: あたし、何もやったこと ないのに‥‥！
English: Like I was some kind of criminal! Can you believe it?

Japanese: ま、まあまあ。 出られたんだし、いいだろ？
English: Well, they let you out in the end, didn't they?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Mmm...

Japanese: あ、そういえば‥‥、 保釈金、ありがとね。
English: Oh, that reminds me... Thanks for bail.

Japanese: お礼なら、御剣に言いなよ。
English: Thank Edgeworth.
Huh?

Japanese: あいつが払ってくれたんだよ。
English: He posted bail for you.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんに カンシャしてるってさ。
English: Said he was grateful for what you did.

Japanese: ミツルギさんが‥‥。
English: Mr. Edgeworth did that...?

Japanese: よし！　がんばろうね なるほどくん！
English: I have to make it up to him!

Japanese: ゼッタイ、助けてあげるんだから！
English: We've got to win this case, Nick!

Japanese: どうしたらいいと思う？ これから。
English: What do you think we should do next?

Japanese: 手がかり、少ないもんね。
English: We're kind of lacking in the clues department.

Japanese: 公園に行って、ヒョッシーでも 探そうか。
English: We could go to the park and look for Gourdy.

Japanese: じょ、じょうだんなのに‥‥。
English: I-I was kidding!

Japanese: でも、何かあるとしたら、 あの公園しかないもんな。
English: Still, if there are any clues out there, the park's as good a bet as any.

Japanese: ‥‥行ってみようか。
English: What do you say? Shall we head down there?

Japanese: うん！
English: Sure!

Japanese: 何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Have you noticed anything lately?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 留置所にいて 思ったんだけど‥‥、
English: You know, I did notice one thing while I was here in detention.

Japanese: けっこうここ、あったかくて いいね。キレイだし。
English: It's really pretty comfortable here. It's warm, and they keep it very clean.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことを 聞きたいんじゃないんだ。
English: I meant have you noticed anything about the case...?

Japanese: まあ。 今は、そんなもんかな。
English: Well... Not much more than that, no.

Japanese: （千尋さんのこともあるし、 そっとしておくか‥‥）
English: (She's probably still upset about Mia. I should leave her alone.)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Cell Visitor's Room

Japanese: 御剣は今、取り調べを 受けているらしい。
English: Looks like Edgeworth is in questioning.

Japanese: また、あとで来ようよ。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: ‥‥そうするか‥‥。
English: Guess so...

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: ‥‥昨日ほどではないけど、 今日も警官のすがたが見える。
English: There are fewer than there were yesterday, but the cops are still around in the park.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事も 来ているかもしれないな‥‥。
English: I wonder if Detective Gumshoe is here today?

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: 今日は警察の人、 あんまりいないね‥‥。
English: There aren't many cops around today, are there?

Japanese: 御剣検事の証拠固めを しているんじゃないかな。
English: They're probably back at the precinct, working up the case against Edgeworth.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Mmm...

Japanese: よっ！　アンタら！
English: Hey y'all!

Japanese: あっ！　ナツミさん！
English: Hey, it's Lotta!

Japanese: アンタ。‥‥今日は やってくれたなァ。
English: Y'all really did it today...

Japanese: な、なんですか。 なんか、モンクでも‥‥
English: Wh-what did we do now?

Japanese: ギャクや。
English: Naw, I'm not complaining!

Japanese: ‥‥ウチ、ちょっと ハンセイしてるんや。
English: See, I did a little thinking. A little... self-reflection, you might say.

Japanese: 証人になるって、ホンマ、 セキニン重大なんやな‥‥。
English: I realized that bein' a witness is a mighty big responsibility.

Japanese: それやのにウチ、浮かれて イイカゲンなコト言うてもうて。
English: But I just went up there and started blabbing any old thing that came to mind.

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥。
English: Lotta...

Japanese: でな。‥‥ウチ、ツミほろぼしが したいねん。
English: So, you see, I want to make it up to y'all.

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”‥‥？
English: "Make it up"...?

Japanese: どうでした？　法廷は‥‥。
English: What did you think of the trial?

Japanese: 正直なハナシ、ウチ、 おもしろ半分やったんや。
English: To be honest, I was doin' it half just to say I'd been a witness...

Japanese: いや。ジッサイ、事件を 見たつもりでいたんやけどな。
English: Even though I didn't really see anything.

Japanese: 見てないモンまで、見たと 思い込んでいたんやなァ。
English: I kinda convinced myself I had though...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタには、 メイワクかけたな‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, I know I caused y'all a lot of trouble.

Japanese: むずかしいですよね。 ‥‥記憶って、アイマイだから。
English: Well, memory is a tricky, vague little thing.

Japanese: うん。‥‥いいベンキョウ させてもらったワ。
English: Yeah, I sure know that now.

Japanese: 今度、サツジンを目撃したときは もう、だいじょうぶや。
English: I'll be fine the next time I witness a murder!

Japanese: そうですよね！
English: Right!

Japanese: （ぼくは目撃したくないぞ‥‥）
English: (You mean the FIRST time you witness a murder...)

Japanese: ヒョッシーの方は どうするんですか？
English: What about Gourdy?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Right!

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、ヒョッシーは また、注目を集めたワケやん！
English: Well, the way I figure, the trial's only stoking the flames of Gourdy fever!

Japanese: なんとしてでも、このウチが スクープしたるねん！
English: I'll get my exclusive photos and rocket to stardom!

Japanese: わあ！ ナツミさん、カッコイイ！
English: All right, Lotta! You go, girl!

Japanese: あたしもなろうかな、 すくーぷかめらまん。
English: I wish I could be an investigative photographer too!

Japanese: （まず、霊媒師の方を なんとかしろって‥‥）
English: (Finish your spirit medium training first!)

Japanese: ナツミさん。 “ツミほろぼし”って‥‥？
English: Lotta, what do you mean by "making it up to us"?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Well, ya see...

Japanese: あのな。ウチ、実は 情報があるねん。
English: Actually, I got a bit of information for you.

Japanese: えええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: カルマのオッサンに口止め されてたんやけどナ。
English: That von Karma didn't want me to say nothin' about it.

Japanese: ど、どんな情報なんですか？
English: Wh-what information!?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Now we're getting to the heart of it!

Japanese: そこで、アンタらと “取り引き”しよういうワケや。
English: See, I reckoned we might be able to do ourselves a little "exchange."

Japanese: と、“とりひき”‥‥？
English: E-"exchange"?

Japanese: あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”を するんでしたよね‥‥？
English: I thought this was to "make it up to us"...?

Japanese: そうやッ！
English: Right!

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”するために “取り引き”するんや！
English: I propose a little exchange, to make it up to you!

Japanese: なんやッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 情報は高いねんで！ タダなんて、マッピラや！
English: Information don't come cheap, my friend!

Japanese: ううう。
English: Uh...

Japanese: コラ、そこ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: “あら、カンサイ人てケチですね” みたいなカオして！
English: I see you thinking "my, how unsophisticated these southern folks are"!!!
It's written all over yer face!

Japanese: ゆうとくけど、 カンサイ人は、ケチちゃうで！
English: Let me tell you, most southerners are WAY more sophisticated than you...

Japanese: ウチが個人的にケチなだけや！
English: I'm just the exception, okay?

Japanese: さー、どうするんや！ ウチと取り引き、するんか！
English: Well, what'll it be? We gonna deal, or not!?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうするの？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Wh-what do we do, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥すみませんが。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: ぼくは、 取り引きする気はありません！
English: But I've no intention of dealing with you!

Japanese: それに、お金もありませんし。
English: Nor do we have any money, anyway!

Japanese: アホやなァ、アンタら。
English: You guys make a fine team, you know that?

Japanese: カネがないことぐらい、 カオ見りゃわかるて。
English: And I can tell you don't have any money, just look at those clothes!

Japanese: （他に手がかりがないんだ、 しかたないな‥‥）
English: (We don't have any other leads so I don't think we have a choice here...)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥いくらですか？
English: Okay. How much?
Huh?

Japanese: ア、アホ言うたらアカン。
English: You completely off your rocker?

Japanese: いくらウチでも、カネは とらへん。
English: I may not be sophisticated, but I'm not trying to rob the poor!
Huh?

Japanese: “情報”と取り引きするには、 やっぱり“情報”や。
English: The only fair exchange for information is... information!

Japanese: ええか。
English: Listen good--

Japanese: アンタらが、ウチにくれるのは、 “ヒョッシー”に関する情報や。
English: What I need from you is information about Gourdy!

Japanese: ひょひょひょ、 “ひょっしー”ですか！
English: Whoa-whoa-whoa! G-Gourdy!?

Japanese: ‥‥そんな！ いないかもしれないのに！
English: B-but Gourdy doesn't... I mean, Gourdy might not exist!

Japanese: いないなら、いないという証拠を 持って来ィ！
English: Then bring me proof that shows he don't!
Uh...

Japanese: ほな、ウチはクルマの中で 張り込みしてるから。
English: I'll be keepin' watch from the car, okay?

Japanese: 何か見つけて、ゼッタイ 来るんやで。ええな！
English: You see something, y'all come to me first, got it?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: O-okay...

Japanese: ん。‥‥ほな！
English: Right! See y'all later!

Japanese: よし！　なるほどくん！ 探すよ！
English: Okay, Nick, let's get hunting!

Japanese: さ、探すって、まさか‥‥。
English: H-hunting...? You don't seriously mean...

Japanese: ヒョッシーに決まってるでしょ！
English: Gourdy? I sure do!

Japanese: み、御剣はどうするんだよ‥‥。
English: What about Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 御剣さんのために、 ヒョッシーを探すの！
English: We're searching for Gourdy for him, Nick! Don't you get it!?

Japanese: （探すっていっても‥‥ どうすればいいんだ‥‥？）
English: (Okay... and how exactly do we search for a make- believe monster...?)

Japanese: （探しものの専門家に 意見を聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe we can find a monster myth specialist?)

Japanese: もう少し、マイクの感度を 上げよう思ォてんねん。
English: I'm thinking I should up the sensitivity of my camera mic.

Japanese: だから、あのへんウロつくときは、 クシャミしたらアカンで！
English: Y'all just be sure y'all don't sneeze or nothing when you're in my part of the woods!

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: Right...

Japanese: やっぱ、拡大しといて 正解やったなァ。
English: I'm glad I did that enlargement.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。
English: No kidding.

Japanese: その写真がなかったら、 有罪になっていましたからね。
English: If we didn't have that, Edgeworth would be in prison now, or worse!

Japanese: なるほどくん、毎回 ドラマチックだからねー。
English: There's never a lack of drama when you're on a case, is there, Nick?

Japanese: アンタ、毎回そんな ガケップチでやってるん？
English: What, are you always on the verge of horrible failure like this!?

Japanese: はあ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥早死にするで。 気ィつけな。
English: Careful or the stress'll send you to an early grave.

Japanese: （よけいなおせわだよ！）
English: (Thanks for the cheerful message of support!)

Japanese: あの。あの。 前から思ってたんですけど。
English: Umm... uh... You know, I was wondering...

Japanese: なんや？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: こんな写真が出ちゃったら、
English: What if you get a picture of Gourdy...

Japanese: 今さら“すくーぷ”には ならないんじゃあ‥‥？
English: and it turns out like this enlargement did?

Japanese: アホタレ！
English: Y'all crazy!?

Japanese: こーんなボヤけた写真、 ウチは撮らへん！
English: A blurry picture like this doesn't make a hit story!

Japanese: ダレが見ても“ヒョッシーやん！” て叫ぶような写真！
English: I need a picture that screams "I am Gourdy! Hear me roar!"

Japanese: それがスクープなんや！
English: I can't turn something like this into the paper! What kind of a fool do y'all think I am?

Japanese: （誰が撃ったかわからないような 証拠写真を撮っておいて）
English: (The kind of fool that would turn a blurry picture in as evidence in a murder trial?)

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (Not to mention claiming she saw something she didn't see!)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: ‥‥今日は矢張のヤツ、 来てないな‥‥。
English: Haven't seen Larry around today at all.

Japanese: カズミさんと、カネのかかる デートでもしているのかな？
English: Probably off paying through the nose on a date with the lovely Kiyance...

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: な‥‥なんだ、ありゃあ‥‥。
English: Wh-what's that?

Japanese: ト‥‥トノサマン、だよね。
English: Th-the Steel Samurai, Nick.

Japanese: よっ！　真宵ちゃん。
English: Yo! Maya!

Japanese: おい、矢張。 ‥‥なんだよアレ！
English: Larry! What the heck is this!?

Japanese: いやー、カズミだよ、カズミ。 オレのカノジョ。
English: Oh, it was my girl Kiyance's idea...

Japanese: “これ置くとォ、インパクト？ あるしィ、売り上げが‥‥”って、
English: She was all "if you like, put this here, it would be, like, really cool!"

Japanese: ノボリといっしょに プレゼントしてくれたんだよ！
English: Dude, she gave it to me along with the banner!

Japanese: すごーい！
English: Wow!

Japanese: カズミさんて、なんでも できるんですねー！
English: That's real impressive she could find those for you!

Japanese: カノジョ、カオが 広いからさ。
English: Well, she knows a lot of people.

Japanese: あの番組、終わったらしくて。 タダでもらってきたんだとさ。
English: And that show's finished now, so she got 'em for free.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: ‥‥あのね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick...
Hmm?

Japanese: なんか、さっきから ミサイルのようすがヘンなの。
English: Missile's been acting strangely...

Japanese: “みさいる”？　‥‥ああ、あの たよりなさそうな小動物か。
English: "Missile"? Oh, oh right.
That little... creature of yours.

Japanese: お。‥‥オレ、子犬って ダイスキなんだよな。
English: Hey, I love lil' doggies!

Japanese: よしよし‥‥。
English: Good boy, good boy.

Japanese: ぐるるるるるるるるるるるる‥‥
English: Grrraaaaaaarh!

Japanese: ど、どうしたの、ミサイル？
English: Wh-what's wrong, Missile?

Japanese: わわわわわわわわわわわわわわわわ わわわわわわわわわわわわわわん！
English: Yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip!

Japanese: きゃあああっ！
English: Eeeeek!

Japanese: ミサイル！　みさいるぅぅ！
English: Missile! Missssssssssile!

Japanese: わぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわ ぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐ
English: Munch munch munch munch munch munch munch munch!

Japanese: わぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわ ぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐ
English: Munch munch munch munch munch munch munch munch!

Japanese: うわわわっ！　やめろ！　やめろ！ オレのトノサマンジュウがああっ！
English: Wh-whoa! S-stop that thing! C-cannibal! It's eating my Samurai Dogs!

Japanese: ‥‥オレの‥‥ トノサマンジュウが‥‥。
English: My Samurai Dogs... *sniff*

Japanese: スゴい‥‥。 ぜーんぶ食べちゃった‥‥。
English: Wow. He ate every single one.

Japanese: ゴメンな、矢張。
English: I'm sorry, Larry...

Japanese: ゴメンですむかッ！
English: "Sorry"!? "Sorry" don't pay my bills, Nick!

Japanese: ははは‥‥。 （ゴメンですますけどな‥‥）
English: Ah hah hah... (It's going to have to this time...)

Japanese: おい、成歩堂！
English: Yo, Nick!

Japanese: 御剣はどうなったんだよ！
English: What happened with Edgeworth?

Japanese: 今日の法廷は、なんとか 逃げ切ったけど‥‥、
English: Well, we made it through the first day in court all right...

Japanese: まだ、なんとも言えないな‥‥。
English: I don't know how good our prospects are from here on, though.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。
English: Huh.

Japanese: ねえ、ねえ、知ってました？
English: Hey, Larry, did you know Edgeworth's secret weakness?

Japanese: 御剣検事って、地震が ニガテなんですよ！
English: He's terrified of earthquakes!

Japanese: コドモみたいですよねー。
English: He acts like a little boy!
Huh?

Japanese: おかしいな‥‥。
English: That's weird.

Japanese: ムカシは、そんなこと なかったけどなあ。
English: I don't think he was ever like that in school.

Japanese: あれ‥‥そうなんですか？
English: No? Really?

Japanese: まあ‥‥アイツとのつきあい、 １年ぐらいだからな。
English: Well, we were only in the same class for a year.

Japanese: すぐに、転校して いっちまったから‥‥。
English: He transferred schools pretty quickly...

Japanese: 転校‥‥。
English: Transferred?

Japanese: （“ＤＬ６号事件”が 起こったからだ‥‥）
English: (Right... when the DL-6 Incident happened.)

Japanese: （矢張も、あの事件のことは 知らないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Doesn't look like Larry knows about it, though.)

Japanese: おい、矢張。
English: Hey, Larry.

Japanese: あの目ざわりなシロモノは、 いつから‥‥？
English: What was that big... thing up there before?

Japanese: ああ、あのデカいヤツか？
English: Huh? Oh, the big guy?

Japanese: そうだな。もう１ヶ月ぐらいに なるかな‥‥。
English: I've had that for about a month, yeah.

Japanese: 子供たちには、けっこう ウケがいいんだぜ。
English: It's a big hit with the kids!

Japanese: じゃあ、なんで昨日は なかったんだ？
English: Why wasn't it there yesterday?

Japanese: え！　ええ？　いや、その。
English: Huh? Huh!? Oh... right.

Japanese: コ‥‥コンプレッサーが こわれちまってサ。
English: Th-the compressor was busted.

Japanese: こんぷれっさー？
English: Compressor?

Japanese: ホレ、屋台の横にあるだろ？ 小さなキカイが。
English: Yeah--it's that little unit by my hotdog stand.

Japanese: あれを使って 空気を入れるんだけどサ。
English: That what I use to put air in the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: ちょっと前にコワれちまって。 修理に出してたんだよ。
English: It broke a little while ago so I sent it in for repairs.

Japanese: なんだ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: ヤッパリさんが ふくらませたんじゃないんだ。
English: And here I thought you'd inflated it by yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: トノサマンをくっつけたボンベが 飛んだのはわかったんですけど、
English: So, the tank and the Steel Samurai you were trying to fill up flew away...

Japanese: ‥‥それから、 どうなったんですか？
English: What happened next?

Japanese: アレが起こったのが、 ２０日ゴロでさ。
English: Well, all that happened on the 20th or so.

Japanese: （２０日‥‥。 今から、約１週間前か‥‥）
English: (The 20th... a week ago.)

Japanese: どうもボンベのヤツ、湖の方へ 飛んでいったみたいだから、
English: Now, as far as I could see, the tank went flying out into the lake.

Japanese: それから毎晩、ボートで 必死に探したんだよ。
English: So I went out every night in a boat looking for it.

Japanese: なんたって、カズミがくれた 愛のトノサマンだからさァ。
English: I mean, Kiyance gave me that Steel Samurai after all!

Japanese: で、いつ見つけたんですか？
English: And when did you find it?

Japanese: ああ。やっと、おとといの夜さ。
English: Just the night before last!

Japanese: スゲェとおくまで飛んでたよ。 探すのに４日もかかっちまった。
English: It flew way out there! Took me four whole days to find it.

Japanese: （おとといの夜‥‥って、 事件があった夜じゃないか！）
English: (The night before last... was the night of the murder!)

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメンな、成歩堂。
English: Sorry for not telling you, Nick.

Japanese: 事件の夜、じつはオレ、ここに いたんだよ。
English: Actually, I was here on the night of the murder.

Japanese: でもな。オレ、１２時より前に 帰っちまったから‥‥。
English: But, you see, I went home before midnight.

Japanese: 湖で事件があったことは 知らないんですか‥‥。
English: So you didn't know about what happened?

Japanese: まあね。
English: No...

Japanese: うーん‥‥ザンネン。
English: That's too bad...

Japanese: でもさ。
English: It's not all bad.

Japanese: どうやら、ヤッカイなナゾが１つ、 カイケツしたみたいだな‥‥。
English: We've solved one mystery at least.

Japanese: ヤッカイなナゾ‥‥？
English: A mystery...?

Japanese: （報告しに行くか‥‥彼女に）
English: (Maybe we should go tell her...)

Japanese: ヤッパリさん。 ホラ、ミサイル。
English: Hey Larry! Look, it's Missile!

Japanese: カワイイでしょ。
English: Isn't he cute?

Japanese: そ、そのイヌを近づけるな！
English: K-keep that mutt away from me!

Japanese: 親方に、なんて 言えばいいんだよ‥‥
English: What am I going to tell the big boss?

Japanese: 今どき、マンジュウ屋で “親方”っていうのも、
English: There's a "big boss" in charge of your hot dog stand?

Japanese: フゼイがあっていいねえ、 なるほどくん。
English: Nick! Maybe the stand is a front for a mafia money laundering scheme!!!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ちょっとは“すみません”ぽい カオをした方がいいよ。
English: I think you should probably try to look a little sorry about what happened.

Japanese: はあい！
English: Oh, right!

Japanese: ‥‥とほほ。 オレのマンジュウよぉ‥‥。
English: Heh... My poor dogs...

Japanese: コイツのおかげで、 マンジュウも売れてるよ。
English: That's the guy that's selling my dogs faster than I can cook 'em!

Japanese: ヒョッシー、ホントに いるのかなあ。
English: Do you think Gourdy really exists?

Japanese: たぶん、何かの見間違いだと 思うけどサ。
English: Nah, I think somebody probably saw something else that they just thought was Gourdy.

Japanese: その正体がわかるまで、 マンジュウを売りまくるぞ！
English: But I'll keep selling Samurai Dogs until the truth's out!

Japanese: ‥‥この湖、魚は ほとんど釣れないぜ。
English: You know, you can't catch many fish in this lake.

Japanese: ザコに用はないんだよ。
English: I'm not after small fry.

Japanese: あたしのエモノは、ズバリ “ヒョッシー”だからね！
English: I'm after the biggest fry of them all... Gourdy!

Japanese: ホンキで釣る つもりなのか‥‥？
English: You really going to try to fish out a monster?

Japanese: 御剣さんを助けるためだよ！
English: To save Edgeworth, yes!

Japanese: 泣かせるなあ。 ‥‥いろんなイミで。
English: Brings a tear to my eye, in more ways than one.

Japanese: なんだよ。 ‥‥ボンベ、か‥‥？
English: What? An air tank?

Japanese: それがどうかしたか？
English: What about it?

Japanese: 矢張。 このボンベだけど‥‥、
English: Larry... I wanted to ask you about this tank...

Japanese: 見おぼえがないか？ ‥‥このボンベ。
English: Have you seen this before?

Japanese: え。‥‥いや。 知らねェぞオレは。
English: Huh? Me? N-no. Never.

Japanese: ‥‥このサムいのに、 ダイビングなんかしないって。
English: Who the heck would go diving in the middle of the winter!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、目がオヨいでるぞ）
English: (There's something about the way his eye twitched when I showed him the tank...)

Japanese: （もっと追求してみた方が よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I think I'd better pursue this line of questioning a little further...)

Japanese: このボンベ‥‥ お前のじゃないか？
English: Say... is this air tank yours?

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでだよ！
English: Wh-why would I have a thing like that?

Japanese: ホラ、ボンベの栓のところに、 万国旗がからまっているだろ。
English: Look--see how there's a string of flags around the tank valve?

Japanese: うしろのトノサマンの万国旗と 似ているからさ。
English: It's just like the string of flags around your Steel Samurai there.

Japanese: ‥‥グ‥‥グーゼンだろ。
English: M-must be a coincidence!

Japanese: 万国旗なんて、どこにでも あるからな‥‥
English: There're strings of flags everywhere these days!

Japanese: ホレ。小学校とか‥‥中学校とか。
English: L-like elementary schools! A-and used car dealerships!

Japanese: だいいち、ボンベなんて、 オレが何に使うって言うんだよ！
English: L-look, why would I need a tank anyway!?

Japanese: お前、これを使って 湖にもぐったんじゃないか？
English: You used this to go diving in the lake, didn't you?

Japanese: な、な、な、なんだって！
English: Wh-wh-what!?

Japanese: なんでオレがそんなこと しなくちゃならねェんだよ！
English: Why the heck would I go diving in the lake!?

Japanese: あッ！
English: Wait!

Japanese: まさかお前‥‥、
English: You... you're not...

Japanese: オレが何か、あの殺人事件と カンケイがあるとでも‥‥！
English: You're not saying I had something to do with that murder!?

Japanese: い、いや、 そういうワケじゃあ‥‥。
English: N-no, not at all, just...

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かねぇぞ！ べ、弁護士を呼んでやる！
English: Hey, you watch it buddy! I'm not saying anything until my lawyer gets here!

Japanese: なるほどくん。弁護士だって！
English: Larry... Nick is your lawyer.

Japanese: なんだ？　矢張。
English: Well, Larry? She's right.

Japanese: フザけんなッ！
English: Bah!

Japanese: と、とにかく、そんなボンベ 知らねェや！
English: A-anyway, I never seen that air tank! Okay?

Japanese: （‥‥ホントかぁ‥‥？）
English: (Yeah, right...)

Japanese: （このタイド、ゼッタイ 何かありそうだぞ‥‥）
English: (Larry is hiding something, I can tell...)

Japanese: お前、これを使って ふくらませたんじゃないのか？
English: You used this to inflate that, didn't you?

Japanese: な‥‥何をだよ！
English: I-inflate what!?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ？
English: What else?

Japanese: うしろでイバってる、 あのトノサマンだよ。
English: That big puffy Steel Samurai!

Japanese: そ、そんなことを聞いて、 どうしようってんだよ。
English: N-now why would you go asking me a question like that.

Japanese: （どうやら、図星らしいな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I hit the nail on the head.)

Japanese: ま‥‥まあ、な。
English: Right... right...

Japanese: 実はさァ。
English: Actually, umm...

Japanese: いつも使ってるコンプレッサーが コワれちまってよ。
English: See, the compressor I always use was on the fritz.

Japanese: １回、ためしに使って みたんだけど‥‥
English: So I tried using the tank to inflate it, just once.

Japanese: ダメ。うまくいかなかったよ。
English: And, er, it didn't go so well.

Japanese: （やっぱりそうだったか‥‥）
English: (As I suspected!)

Japanese: （まあ、ボンベのことは こんなところでじゅうぶんかな）
English: (I guess I've gotten to the bottom of that mystery!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。やれやれ。
English: Heh. Sorry!

Japanese: “うまくいかなかった”‥‥？
English: It "didn't go so well"?

Japanese: ま、まあな。
English: Er, yeah.

Japanese: その話、もっとくわしく 聞かせてくれないか？
English: Do you think you could be a little more specific?

Japanese: な、なんでよ？
English: C-c'mon...

Japanese: じつはこの話、あんまり したくないんだよなァ‥‥。
English: Look, it's embarrassing so I really don't want to talk about it...

Japanese: えー。聞きたい聞きたい！
English: Tell us! Tell us!

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。
English: Fine.

Japanese: いやナ。さっきも言ったけど、 コンプレッサーがコワれたからさ、
English: Whatever. It's like what I said, the compressor was busted.

Japanese: ボンベの空気でふくらまそうと してみたワケよ。
English: So I took the tank and tried to fill the Samurai up with that.

Japanese: ところが！
English: And then...

Japanese: パアァァン！
English: BLAAAAM!

Japanese: すげェ音がして 栓がハジけ飛んじまってさァ！
English: The valve busted open and made this incredible noise!

Japanese: ボンベのヤツ、ロケットみたいに “しゅぽーん！”って‥‥
English: And that tank there took off like a rocket.

Japanese: しわしわのトノサマンくっつけて、 飛んでいっちまったんだよ！
English: And it took my poor deflated Steel Samurai with it!

Japanese: ええーっ！
English: What!? Off into Gourd Lake?

Japanese: そりゃあすげェ音がして、 オレもビビっちまったよ！
English: It sure scared me out of my gourd, that's for sure.

Japanese: そのボンベで、トノサマンを ふくらまそうとしてサ。
English: See, I tried to use this tank to fill up the Samurai.

Japanese: トノサマンにボンベを くっつけた、まさにそのとき！
English: I was attaching the tank, when it happened!

Japanese: パアァァン！
English: BLAAAAM!

Japanese: 栓がハジけ飛んじまってさァ！
English: The valve just busted open and the whole thing took off!

Japanese: ボンベのヤツ、ロケットみたいに “しゅぽーん！”って‥‥
English: The tank shot up in the air like a rocket...

Japanese: しわしわのトノサマンくっつけて、 飛んでいっちまったんだよ！
English: And it took my poor deflated Steel Samurai with it!

Japanese: ありゃあ、スゲェ光景だったね。
English: Man, what a sight that was.

Japanese: あたしも見たかった！
English: I wish I could have seen it!

Japanese: ぼくもだ。
English: Me too.

Japanese: 悪いな、成歩堂。
English: Sorry, Nick.

Japanese: コドクなマンジュウ売りにゃ、 なんのことかわかんねえよ。
English: I don't know much about that. I am a mere seller of hotdogs.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 26 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: （今日もボート屋は 休みみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like the boat rental shop is closed today, too.)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 26 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: ここって、いつも しずかだねえ。
English: It's always so quiet, here.

Japanese: ボート屋さん、 やってないのかな。
English: I wonder if the boat shop is closed for good?

Japanese: 湖で殺人があった わけだから、
English: Well, with the murder on the lake and all...

Japanese: しばらく休むんじゃないかな。
English: They're probably just taking a vacation till it blows over.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I get it.

Japanese: ピー　ピー　ピー
English: *beep* *beep* *beep*

Japanese: な！　なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick!

Japanese: 反応してるよ！ き、金属探知機がッ！
English: It's beeping! The metal detector's found something!

Japanese: ‥‥き、近所メイワクな 探知機だな‥‥。
English: Sure is loud enough about it.

Japanese: （どうやら、奥のシゲミに 反応しているみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, it must be in those bushes...)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。ちょっと見てきて。
English: Go check it out, Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥なんであたしが‥‥。
English: Why do I have to check it out?

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick.

Japanese: こんなものが。
English: Look!

Japanese: ‥‥それって‥‥ ボンベ、だよね。
English: Huh...? An air tank?

Japanese: 栓の部分がこわれてるみたいだ。
English: Huh. The valve looks broken.

Japanese: ヒョッシーじゃなかったね。
English: I thought it was Gourdy...

Japanese: いいかい真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya, first of all...

Japanese: まず、ヒョッシーは シゲミにはいないんだ。
English: Why would Gourdy be in the bushes!?

Japanese: そして、ヒョッシーは、 金属探知機には反応しないよ。
English: And second of all, why would a metal detector react to a sea monster!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Oh.
Huh?

Japanese: （‥‥このボンベ‥‥ 何かからみついてるな‥‥）
English: (There's something wrapped around this air tank...)
(It looks like... a string of flags...?)

Japanese: いちおう、ひろっておこうか。 せっかくだし。
English: Well, we might as well take it with us now that we've found it.

Japanese: （‥‥重いな）
English: (It's heavy...)

Japanese: 役に立つのかわからないが、 《ボンベ》をひろった。
English: Picked up air tank of dubious value.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 26 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: ナツミさんが言ってたの、 あそこのボート屋さんだよね。
English: This is the boat shop that Lotta was talking about!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: You're right.

Japanese: でも、人のケハイなんか しないぞ‥‥。
English: Doesn't seem to be anyone around at all.

Japanese: ま。ま。 とにかく、行ってみようよ。
English: Well, let's go check it out anyway!

Japanese: ‥‥なんでサユリさん、 ＤＬ６号事件のことを‥‥。
English: Why would that bird Polly know about DL-6...?

Japanese: あのオジさんの正体を 考えなきゃいけないな。
English: We have to figure out who that old man is.

Japanese: あ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: どうした？
English: What?

Japanese: オジさん、カギかけちゃった。 ドアの向こうから。
English: He locked the door, from the other side.

Japanese: （‥‥あのオジさん、 ナニモノなんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Who could that old man be...?)

Japanese: （もっと知る必要があるな‥‥ ＤＬ６号事件のこと）
English: (I think I need to do a little more research on this DL-6 Incident...)

Japanese: （聞いてみるか。 イトノコ刑事にでも‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should ask Detective Gumshoe.)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: あっ。イトノコ刑事。
English: Hey, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: おお！
English: Hey, pal!

Japanese: 今日の法廷は、‥‥そのう‥‥。
English: The trial today, it, er...

Japanese: どうしました？
English: Yes? What about the trial?

Japanese: イヤ。“おミゴトでした”と 言うほどアザヤカでもなく、
English: Well, I was going to say "good show," but it wasn't really all that...

Japanese: とはいえ、いちおう検事を 助けてくれているし‥‥
English: Though you did save Edgeworth, I guess...

Japanese: なんとお礼をすればいいか、 コトバに詰まったッス。
English: I just wasn't sure how to thank you... you know?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Er... thanks.

Japanese: では、自分は一度、 署にもどるッス。
English: I'm going to head back to the station.

Japanese: 真宵さんの調書をとって、 すぐに保釈の手続きをするッス。
English: I'll get the report on Maya and get her out of there as soon as I can.

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥おねがいします。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: あ！　そうだ。
English: Oh, wait!

Japanese: そのー‥‥保釈金って、 いくらぐらいなんですか？
English: Umm... I was wondering, how much is bail going to be?

Japanese: シンパイいらねッス。
English: Don't worry about that.

Japanese: 保釈金は全額、御剣検事が 支払うッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth is posting the whole amount.

Japanese: え‥‥、御剣が‥‥？
English: What? Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 言ったはずッス。
English: Didn't I tell you?

Japanese: 検事は、彼女に カンシャしてるッス。
English: He's grateful to her for what she did.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、あとで彼女を むかえに来るッス！
English: Alright, pal. Well don't forget to go pick her up, okay?

Japanese: （今月の家賃も、全額 支払ってくれ‥‥）
English: (Maybe I can get Edgeworth to pay this month's rent, too...)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: 狩魔検事、 明日はどういう作戦で‥‥？
English: Any idea what strategy von Karma is planning for tomorrow?

Japanese: どうやら、もう１人の 目撃証人を立てるらしいッス。
English: It sounds like he's bringing in another witness!

Japanese: “もう１人の目撃証人”‥‥？
English: Another witness!?

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、今日の法廷で、
English: Oh, right, he said something about that in the trial today...

Japanese: 目撃者は、２人いたのだ。
English: There were two witnesses!

Japanese: ‥‥とか言ってましたっけ。
English: I was wondering who that other witness was!

Japanese: で‥‥、どういう証人なんですか？
English: Er... who was it?

Japanese: す‥‥すまないッスが‥‥、
English: S... sorry, pal.

Japanese: さすがにその情報は、 もらせないッス。
English: As much as I'd like to, I'm not at liberty to divulge that information.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥）
English: (Right...)

Japanese: そういえば、 御剣検事ですけど‥‥。
English: Oh, right... I wanted to ask you something about Edgeworth.

Japanese: なんスか？
English: What's up?

Japanese: 彼、地震がニガテなんですか？ 聞いたこと、なかったんですけど。
English: Is he afraid of earthquakes? I never heard anything about that before.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、自分の話は ほとんどしないッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth doesn't talk about himself too much, see.

Japanese: でも、これだけは間違いないッス。
English: But there's one thing that's clear as day...

Japanese: あの人が、あんなに犯罪を ニクむようになったのも、
English: Him hating crime the way he does...

Japanese: 検事になることを決めたのも、
English: And him becoming a lawyer...

Japanese: そして、地震を死ぬほど 怖がるようになったのも‥‥、
English: And him being scared of earthquakes...

Japanese: すべては、あの事件が キッカケになっているッス。
English: It all started with that incident.

Japanese: ‥‥“ＤＬ６号事件”ですか？
English: The DL-6 Incident?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yep, that's the one.

Japanese: １５年前‥‥。検事は、目の前で お父さんを‥‥。
English: Fifteen years ago... when he saw his father shot before his very eyes!

Japanese: ‥‥あの人は今でも、 苦しんでいるッス。
English: He still feels the pain now, you can see it in his eyes.

Japanese: ウチの綾里　真宵の ことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I wanted to talk to you about Maya Fey...

Japanese: あれ？　まだ釈放されて ないッス？
English: Huh? She's not out on bail yet?

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいッスね。
English: That's strange.

Japanese: 調書をとったら、すぐ 帰らせるように言ったッスが。
English: I told 'em to let her go as soon as they had their report written up.

Japanese: でも。
English: Man...

Japanese: ‥‥今日は、彼女の おかげで助かったッス。
English: I don't know what would have happened in that courtroom today if it weren't for her.

Japanese: 係官にハガイじめされて 引きずり出される彼女を見て、
English: Seeing her getting dragged out by the bailiff...

Japanese: 自分は正直、ナミダが 止まらなかったッス。
English: I'll be honest with you, pal. I shed a tear or two.

Japanese: 御剣検事も、カンドーして クチビルをふるわせていたッス。
English: Edgeworth, he was so moved I saw his lip trembling.

Japanese: ほ、ほんとですか！ （あの御剣が‥‥？）
English: Really!? (Cold-as-ice Edgeworth!?)

Japanese: 検事は、ホントにカンシャ してたッス。
English: He was really grateful for what she did, you know.

Japanese: それをホコらしげに 見せるのは、まだ早いッス。
English: I think it's a little early for you to be proudly waving that thing around.

Japanese: ムザイ判決をもらったとき、 自分はアンタを認めるッス！
English: You can be proud of yourself when you get Mr. Edgeworth declared innocent, pal!

Japanese: ジッサイ、あの女には やられたッス。
English: That woman really took us for a ride, didn't she?

Japanese: まったく、人さわがせな‥‥。
English: Claiming she was a witness to a murder and all...

Japanese: でも、彼女の写真があったから、 御剣は助かったんですよ。
English: Still, if it weren't for her photograph, Mr. Edgeworth wouldn't have made it today.

Japanese: あ、あれ。そうだったッスか。
English: Oh, huh, really?

Japanese: （裁判、ちゃんと聞いてたのか この刑事‥‥）
English: (Umm... you were at the trial, weren't you, Detective?)

Japanese: 生倉　雪夫‥‥。
English: Robert Hammond...

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の弁護士ッス。
English: He was the defense attorney in the DL-6 Incident trial.

Japanese: たった１人の容疑者を、 ムザイにしたオトコッス。
English: He's the one who got the sole suspect off the hook.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、コイツのことを どう思ってるッスかねえ‥‥。
English: I wonder what Mr. Edgeworth thinks of him. Not much, I'd wager.

Japanese: こんなの、誰が見ても 御剣検事じゃないッスよね！
English: I don't care who you are, no one can tell me that that's Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: だいいち、 ゼンゼン、似てないッス！
English: I mean, come on, it doesn't look like him at all!

Japanese: でも御剣は、このボートに乗って いたことは、認めてるんですよ。
English: But Edgeworth has admitted he was on the boat.

Japanese: じゃあ、こっちの撃たれている 方が、御剣検事ッス！
English: Then he's the one being shot, then!

Japanese: でも、彼はピンピンして 被告席に立っていますけどね。
English: He seemed fine in court today...
Details, pal, details!

Japanese: アンタ、どっちの味方ッスか！
English: Whose side are you on, anyway?

Japanese: （証拠から目をそらしたって、 しかたないってことなのに‥‥）
English: (All I'm saying is we have to respect the evidence...)

Japanese: こんなの、誰が見ても 御剣検事じゃないッスよね！
English: I don't care who you are, no one can tell me that that's Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: だいいち、 ゼンゼン、似てないッス！
English: I mean, come on, it doesn't look like him at all!

Japanese: でも御剣は、このボートに乗って いたことは、認めてるんですよ。
English: But Edgeworth has admitted he was on the boat.

Japanese: じゃあ、こっちの撃たれている 方が、御剣検事ッス！
English: Then he's the one being shot, then!

Japanese: でも、彼はピンピンして 被告席に立っていますけどね。
English: He seemed fine in court today...
Details, pal, details!

Japanese: アンタ、どっちの味方ッスか！
English: Whose side are you on, anyway?

Japanese: （証拠から目をそらしたって、 しかたないってことなのに‥‥）
English: (All I'm saying is we have to respect the evidence...)

Japanese: 間違いなく、凶器から発射 されているッス。
English: Yep, it was fired from the murder weapon, without a doubt.

Japanese: １ｍの距離から撃たれたとなると、 ボートの上しか考えられない‥‥。
English: From a meter away... so it would have to have been from someone else on the boat.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どう思います？　イトノコ刑事。
English: What do you make of this, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: それを考えるのが、 アンタの仕事ッス！
English: Hey, making something of things is your job!

Japanese: はあ。
English: Er... right.

Japanese: （‥‥って、それは刑事の シゴトだろう！）
English: (Since when do lawyers have to do detectives' work for them!?)

Japanese: なんで御剣検事の指紋が ついてるッスかね‥‥。
English: Maybe you should tell me why Mr. Edgeworth's prints were on the weapon, pal.

Japanese: そんなの、ぼくに わかるワケ、ないでしょう！
English: Huh? How should I know!?

Japanese: 彼女は、２発、銃声を 聞いているッス。
English: Now, she heard two gunshots, right?

Japanese: でも‥‥ 法廷記録によると、
English: But wait... Look at the Court Record!

Japanese: 凶器のピストルは、 ３発撃たれているんですよ。
English: According to this, the murder weapon was fired three times!

Japanese: な！　なんスと！
English: What!? How's that possible!?

Japanese: 残りの１発は どこへ行ったんでしょうね？
English: Where was the third shot fired?

Japanese: さ、さあ‥‥。
English: I wonder...

Japanese: ためし撃ちでも したッスかねえ‥‥。
English: Maybe it was a practice shot or something?

Japanese: そんな、期待にみちた目で 見られてもコマるッス。
English: Sorry to disappoint you, pal.

Japanese: なんにも思い浮かばねッス。
English: I'm drawing a blank.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: おっ！　アンタら。
English: Hey y'all!

Japanese: どうや。‥‥ヒョッシーのコト、 何かわかったんか？
English: Well? Y'all find anything out about Gourdy?

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、 まだ見つからないんですよ。
English: Umm... no, nothing.

Japanese: 気合い入れなアカンで。 この辺、夜は冷え込むからなぁ。
English: Well, keep moving! It gets cold out here at nighttime.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥サムいですね。
English: It is a little chilly...

Japanese: あ‥‥クシャミが‥‥。
English: I... I think I have to sneeze!

Japanese: ア、アカン！ クシャミはアカンでぇぇぇ！
English: Wh-whoa! No you don't! No sneezing!!!

Japanese: ハックショ！
English: Whaaa-cho!

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: コラァ！　クシャミはアカン 言うたろぉが！
English: I told y'all NO SNEEZING!

Japanese: 自動シャッターの“破裂音”の 設定を、少しユルめたんや。
English: See, I set the camera to respond to things a little softer than a "bang."

Japanese: 今、カメラはカルマ検事の 指パッチンにも反応するんや！
English: It'd trigger on one of von Karma's finger snaps now!

Japanese: ううう‥‥スミマセン。
English: Oh... I'm sorry.

Japanese: どぉぉしてくれるんや！ フィルム！
English: Yeah, well, sorry's nice but what about my film!?

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥払っといて。
English: Nick... pay the lady.

Japanese: （‥‥とほほ）
English: (*sob*)

Japanese: もし、ヒョッシーがいなかったら どうするんですか？
English: What are you going to do if Gourdy doesn't exist?

Japanese: スクープカメラマン、やめる。
English: I'll quit being an investigative photographer.

Japanese: ええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: いやな。ウチ、実は今まで、 １枚しか撮ったことがないんや。
English: After all, I only have one photo to my name so far...

Japanese: スクープ写真ですか？
English: Was it a good one?

Japanese: せや。‥‥ＵＦＯの写真 なんやけどな。
English: You bet... a yoofoh!

Japanese: ゆ‥‥ゆーほー。
English: A y-yoofoh...?

Japanese: だからな。‥‥今回、もし ヒョッシーが撮れんかったら‥‥、
English: Anyway, if I can't get a career-making photo this time around...

Japanese: キッパリやめるんや。 そして、大学にもどるワ。
English: Then that's it! I'll quit and go back to school.

Japanese: え！　ナツミさん、ホントに 大学生なんですか？
English: Huh? So you really are a university student?

Japanese: まあな。‥‥休学中や。
English: Yeah, well, I'm takin' a break for a bit.

Japanese: はああ‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: どうやニイちゃん。
English: Well, Mister Lawyer?

Japanese: ウチは待ってんねんで。 アンタの情報をナ。
English: I've got the info y'all need!

Japanese: どうや？　ヒョッシー、 見つかりそうなん？
English: Y'all got the scoop on Gourdy for me yet?

Japanese: そんなにカンタンには 見つかりませんよ。
English: It's not going to be that easy to find him, you know.

Japanese: 早よォせんと、明日の 裁判に間にあわんで。
English: You'd better hurry or you won't have that info in time for the trial tomorrow!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: もう見つけましたよ、 ヒョッシーなら。
English: Huh? Gourdy? Oh, we found him already.

Japanese: えっ！　あたしまだ 見てないよ！
English: What!? I haven't seen any monsters yet!

Japanese: や、やっぱりヒョッシーは いたんや！
English: Y-y'all for real!? Gourdy really exists!?

Japanese: アンタ！
English: Wait!

Japanese: み、見つけたっていう証拠は！ ‥‥写真でも撮ったんか！
English: I need proof! You get a photo!?

Japanese: 証拠はもちろん、ありますよ。
English: Of course I have proof.

Japanese: ‥‥ズルいや、なるほどくん。
English: No fair, Nick!

Japanese: あたしがトイレに行ったときだ。
English: It was when I went to the bathroom, wasn't it!?

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき、ヒョッシーに コンタクトを‥‥。
English: That's when you made contact with Gourdy!

Japanese: ほれ！　ほれ！　ヒョッシーを 見つけた証拠を出しィな！
English: Enough jabbering already, let's see your proof!

Japanese: アンタ、ウチをバカに しとるんか！
English: Y'all trying to make a fool outta me?

Japanese: そんなの、証拠でも なんでもあらへん！
English: That don't prove diddly-squat!

Japanese: うう。あやうくごまかされる ところだったよ‥‥。
English: Hmph. We almost had her duped, too.

Japanese: （‥‥なんとかナットク させてしまいたいんだけど‥‥）
English: (We need to do something soon or I won't get that information off of her!)

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Lotta...

Japanese: ‥‥いないんですよ。 ヒョッシーなんて、ね。
English: There is no such thing as Gourdy.

Japanese: な、なんやて！
English: Wh-what!? How can y'all be so sure!

Japanese: ホ‥‥ホントなの！ なるほどくん！
English: R-really, Nick!?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてや！ 証拠でも、あるんか！
English: Y'all got some proof Gourdy don't exist!?

Japanese: ヒョッシーがいないという 証拠は‥‥
English: The proof that Gourdy doesn't exist...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、証拠はないですけど、 弁護士としての“カン”が‥‥。
English: I don't have it yet, but my lawyer instincts tell me the proof is out there.

Japanese: なんや！　証拠もないのに そんなこと言ったんか！
English: Hah! Nice try, but y'all can't pull the wool over these eyes that easy!

Japanese: 弁護士シッカクだよ！ なるほどくん！
English: That won't work in court, either, Nick...

Japanese: （ホンキで怒られてしまった）
English: (Great, now they're both mad at me.)

Japanese: もちろんあります。
English: Of course I have proof.

Japanese: 弁護士は、証拠なしには ケツロンは出しませんから。
English: No lawyer worth his badge would make a claim without the proof to back it up.

Japanese: ヒョッシーがいないという 証拠は‥‥
English: Here's the proof that Gourdy doesn't exist...

Japanese: なんや！　弁護士は、デタラメな 証拠でケツロンを出すんかい！
English: Hah! All that fancy talk about lawyers and proof had me going for a second there!

Japanese: カオ洗って、出なおして来ィ！
English: Y'all better take a good hard look at that badge of yours and consider a career change!

Japanese: （この証拠でナットクさせるのは ムリか‥‥）
English: (Great, the "investigative photographer" is giving me career advice...)

Japanese: 矢張さんのボンベ‥‥？
English: Larry's air tank...?

Japanese: ‥‥なんや、そのボンベは。
English: What're y'all doing with an air tank?

Japanese: これが、ヒョッシーです。
English: This... is Gourdy.

Japanese: ‥‥はぁ？
English: Umm... scuze me?

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうこと、 なるほどくん？
English: Wh-what exactly are you saying, Nick?

Japanese: この近くに、マンジュウの 屋台があるんですよ。
English: There's a stand near here... a hotdog stand.

Japanese: そこには、空気でふくらませた 大きな人形があるんです。
English: There's a giant inflatable samurai doll there.

Japanese: １週間前、ちょっとドジなヤツが ボンベでそれに空気を‥‥。
English: About a week ago, an idiot, who happens to be a friend of mine, tried to fill it.

Japanese: ボンベの栓は破裂して、 湖の方へ飛んでいきました。
English: He used this air tank, and when the valve blew, the tank flew into the lake.

Japanese: “パァァン！”という 破裂音とともにね。
English: Apparently, it made a pretty loud "bang" when it flew...
A "bang"...?

Japanese: ボンベは、しわしわの人形を つけたまま、湖に着水しました。
English: The tank, along with the still deflated Samurai fell into the lake.

Japanese: この瞬間！
English: At the same time...

Japanese: 偶然、湖のほとりで写真を 撮ったカップルがいたんです。
English: A couple was taking a photograph of the lake.

Japanese: それが、この写真です。
English: This photo!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥
English: Wait...

Japanese: じゃあ、ヒョッシーの正体は‥‥
English: So... you're saying that Gourdy...

Japanese: しぼんだトノサマンだったの！？
English: Is really the Steel Samurai!?

Japanese: ウチのユメもしぼんだわ。
English: Well, that's a fine way to ruin a gal's dreams.

Japanese: すみません‥‥ナツミさん。
English: I'm sorry, Lotta.

Japanese: わかったわ‥‥。 アンタの勝ちや。
English: Nah, it's okay, you win.

Japanese: ヤクソクどおり、情報を 聞かせてやるで‥‥。
English: I'll give you your info, like I promised.

Japanese: ナツミさん、ちょっと カワイソウ‥‥。
English: Poor Lotta...

Japanese: ウチな、今日の裁判で、 つくづく思い知ったんや。
English: I learned something in today's trial, that's for sure.

Japanese: ええかげんなキモチで 証言したらあかんねんな、って。
English: Testifyin' is serious business!

Japanese: だから、もうあの事件のことは しゃべらんことに決めたんや。
English: That's why I decided not to talk about that case any more.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: いやいや。この事件について、何か 情報があるって言ってましたよね。
English: Whoa--didn't you say you had information about the case!?

Japanese: それを教えてくださいよ！
English: Tell us that, at least!

Japanese: だぁかぁらぁ。
English: Like I said...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ヒョッシーの情報と 引きかえや。
English: I'll trade it for the dirt on Gourdy!

Japanese: じゃあ、ナツミさんの知っている “情報”を教えてください。
English: So, tell us this "information" you have.

Japanese: ‥‥しゃあないな。約束やし。
English: A promise is a promise, I guess...

Japanese: 警察で聞いた話やけど、 明日の検察の証人なァ、
English: I overheard the cops around here saying something about the witness tomorrow...

Japanese: この先にある貸しボート屋の 管理人らしいで。
English: They said he's the caretaker of the boat rental place up the path here.

Japanese: 貸しボート屋‥‥？
English: Boat rental...?

Japanese: あそこ、ヒトがいたんですか？
English: There's someone there? I mean, it looks so deserted.

Japanese: オッサンが１人で 住んでるんや。
English: Just an old guy, living all by himself.

Japanese: 調べてみたらええんちゃう？
English: Y'all should go check it out.

Japanese: ありがとう！　ナツミさん！
English: Thanks, Lotta! We will!

Japanese: ホラ、行こうよなるほどくん！
English: Let's get cracking, Nick!

Japanese: ちょっと待ちィ。
English: Hold on.

Japanese: まだ、何かあるんですか？
English: Something else?

Japanese: 事件のあった晩やけどな。
English: Yeah... the night of the murder.

Japanese: ウチのカメラ、 ２回作動してたんや。
English: My camera clicked twice, you know.

Japanese: じゃあ、この写真以外に、 もう１回、撮影を‥‥？
English: Wait... so you have another photo?

Japanese: まあな。
English: Well...

Japanese: ただ‥‥湖には なーんも写っとらん。
English: Yeah, but there's nothing in it at all. Just the lake.

Japanese: なんの証拠にもならん思ォて、 今まで出さなかったんやけど、
English: I figured it wouldn't be much use as evidence, so I kept it to myself.

Japanese: 何がどういう証拠になるか わからんからな。
English: Well, it might not be helpful at all, but...

Japanese: コレ、持って行きィ。
English: Here, take it.

Japanese: 《もう１枚の湖の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Second Lake Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ほな。
English: Bye now. Y'all take care.

Japanese: ウチ、荷物かたづけて 帰るさかい‥‥。
English: Time for me to pack up and leave.

Japanese: ナツミさん、カワイソウ‥‥。
English: Poor Lotta...

Japanese: 矢張のせいだ。
English: It's all Larry's fault.

Japanese: アイツの伝説は、 いまだ健在だったな。
English: The legend still lives on, I guess.

Japanese: “でんせつ”‥‥？
English: The "legend"...?

Japanese: 矢張の関係者はみんな、 この言葉を思い知る運命なんだよ。
English: Yeah, the legend of Larry, familiar to all who know him for any length of time...

Japanese: “事件のカゲに、ヤッパリ矢張” ‥‥ってね。
English: "When something smells, it's usually the Butz."
Hmm.

Japanese: ちょっぴりメイワク、かも。
English: Someone should whip that Butz into shape.

Japanese: ウチ、なんとしてもヒョッシーを 撮らなアカンねん。
English: I gotta do what I gotta do to get me a picture of Gourdy!

Japanese: だから、自動シャッターのマイクの 感度、上げてあるんや。
English: That's why I raised the sensitivity of my mic.

Japanese: 今度こそ、気ィつけてや。
English: Just... watch yourself from now on, okay?

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: Sorry, sorry!

Japanese: 待っててくださいね、ナツミさん。 ‥‥これで、ヒョッシーを‥‥。
English: Lotta, wait... For catching Gourdy...

Japanese: そのツリザオでか！
English: A fishing pole!? Are you out of your doggone mind!?

Japanese: そうです！
English: Yes! I mean... yes, it's a fishing pole!
Huh.

Japanese: そりゃあ思いつかんかった！ ‥‥キタイしとるで！
English: I never thought of that! Good luck!

Japanese: はい！
English: Thanks!

Japanese: （‥‥ウソだろ‥‥）
English: (I don't believe it...)

Japanese: ナツミさん、ホラ。
English: Hey, Lotta!

Japanese: お。カワイイなぁ。 ‥‥アンタの子かいな？
English: Oh, cute! He yours?

Japanese: ケーサツ犬なんですよ！ ミサイルっていうの。
English: He's a K-9 police dog! His name's "Missile."

Japanese: へええ。
English: Huh.

Japanese: でも、警察犬て、何かのニオイを 追いかけるモンやろ？
English: K-9s are the ones they use to sniff for things, right?

Japanese: ヒョッシーって、どんな ニオイなんや？
English: I wonder what Gourdy smells like...

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そこまでは考えてなかったなぁ。
English: I hadn't thought about that.

Japanese: ナツミさん、ホラ。 金属探知機っていうんですよ！
English: Hey, Lotta, look at this: It's a metal detector!

Japanese: 知っとるわ。
English: I know what it is.

Japanese: なあ、アンタ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ヒョッシーは、ピストル持った ハイジャック犯やないんや。
English: I'm not sure that's going to pick up Gourdy, though.

Japanese: 金属探知機じゃァ 引っかからんのとちゃうか？
English: Unless he's been eating people's watches or spare change or something.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: そこまでは考えてなかったなぁ。
English: I hadn't thought about that.

Japanese: 知らん。 ‥‥なーんも知らん。
English: Yep, I'd have to say I know exactly squat about that.

Japanese: （あいかわらず、関心のない ものにはタンパクだな‥‥）
English: (It's painfully easy to know when something doesn't catch Lotta's interest...)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 26 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ いないみたいだな）
English: (Detective Gumshoe's not here...)

Japanese: イトノコなら、今日も 現場に行ってるよ。
English: Gumshoe's at the scene again today.

Japanese: え‥‥あ、そうなんですか。
English: Huh? Oh, really?

Japanese: あいつもバカだよ。
English: He's a live wire that one.

Japanese: 捜査方針に逆らって、 課長と大ゲンカしちゃって。
English: Got into a fight with the chief for not following protocol...

Japanese: （捜査方針‥‥）
English: (Not following protocol...?)

Japanese: （やっぱり、御剣の容疑を 固めようという方向だろうな）
English: (I bet he wouldn't help them build the case against Edgeworth...)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 26 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: おお。アンタッスか。
English: Hey there, pal.

Japanese: どうしたッスか？ むずかしいカオをして。
English: What's up? You look out of sorts.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、また御剣検事に 不利なコトを‥‥！
English: Wait... you didn't go and do something that's going to hurt Mr. Edgeworth's case again!

Japanese: “また”はないでしょう！
English: What do you mean, "again"!?

Japanese: ま、とにかく、そこにすわるッス。
English: Whatever, have a seat, pal.

Japanese: カネのこと以外なら、 相談にのるッス。
English: I'm here for you if you need anything. Besides money, that is.

Japanese: 捜査の方は、どうですか？
English: How is the investigation proceeding?

Japanese: どうもこうもないッス。
English: It's not, really.

Japanese: これからまた、捜査会議が あるッスが‥‥、
English: We have another meeting coming up...

Japanese: 御剣検事の動機をウラづける 捜査のハナシだけッス。
English: We're supposed to talk about Mr. Edgeworth's motive...

Japanese: 動機‥‥ですか。
English: His motive?

Japanese: 御剣検事のお父さんが 亡くなったＤＬ６号事件で、
English: See, Mr. Edgeworth's father died in the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: その容疑者を無罪にしたのが、 被害者の生倉弁護士ッス。
English: And the guy who got the lone suspect declared innocent was the victim in this case...

Japanese: だから御剣検事は、生倉弁護士に フクシュウした‥‥。
English: Robert Hammond.

Japanese: おそらく、そんなケツロンに なると思うッス。
English: They're saying that's why Mr. Edgeworth shot him.

Japanese: （‥‥御剣は、過去を語ろうと しない）
English: (And Edgeworth never talks about his past...)

Japanese: （明日は、ムリヤリその話が 法廷で暴かれるわけか‥‥）
English: (I bet they'll drag that out and hit him with it in court tomorrow, too...)

Japanese: ‥‥御剣さん‥‥。
English: Poor Edgeworth...

Japanese: 正直なところ、 これは‥‥イタイッス‥‥。
English: I gotta admit, it doesn't look good, pal.

Japanese: あの‥‥イトノコ刑事さん。 “ヒョッシー”って知ってますか？
English: Say, Detective Gumshoe. Do you know "Gourdy"?

Japanese: ああ‥‥。ひょうたん湖で 誰かが見たとかいう‥‥？
English: The monster down in Gourd Lake? Not personally, no.

Japanese: あたしたち、今、それを 探しているんですよ。
English: Well... we're looking for him.

Japanese: なッ！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: 何をやっとるッスかッ！
English: Are you out of your minds!?

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: そんなモン探すヒマがあったら、
English: You got time to go wild monster hunting!?

Japanese: 付近のキキコミでも したらどうッスか！
English: How about doing a little questioning for me then!?
Oh...

Japanese: イトノコさん、コワい‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe is scaring me, Nick.

Japanese: ぼくは、ナツミさんの“取り引き” のことを、イトノコ刑事に話した。
English: I told Detective Gumshoe about the deal with Lotta.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ もっと早く話してよ！
English: Nick! Try telling him sooner next time!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメン。
English: Er, sorry.

Japanese: そうだったッスか‥‥。 ドナって、すまなかったッス。
English: I see, pal. Sorry for shoutin' at you.

Japanese: わかったッス！
English: Okay!

Japanese: このイトノコ、いや、警視庁が ヒョッシー探しに協力するッス！
English: I, Detective Gumshoe, will aid your search for Gourdy!

Japanese: えっ！
English: H-huh!?

Japanese: 最新の捜査用のヒミツ兵器を、 トクベツに貸すッス！
English: I'll loan you one of our newest secret weapons for finding evidence!

Japanese: ホントですか！
English: Really!?

Japanese: どれでも、好きなのを 持って行くといいッス！
English: You can take whichever one you like!

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、サッソク 貸してくださいよ！
English: Okay, give us the goods!

Japanese: まあまあ。あわてちゃダメッス。 まず、ヒミツ兵器を紹介するッス。
English: Hold on now, everything in due time. First, let me show 'em to you!

Japanese: では、紹介するッス。警視庁の ホコる、最新鋭の兵器たち。
English: These are our best and brightest!

Japanese: ヒミツ兵器その１。 ‥‥“ミサイル”。
English: Introducing Secret Weapon No. 1: "Missile"!

Japanese: みみ、みさいる？
English: M-M-Missile?

Japanese: ただ今、訓練中の、バリバリの 警察犬ッス。
English: He's a K-9 police dog, still in training!

Japanese: ミサイル、ミサイル。 おいでおいで‥‥。
English: Missile! Missile! Here, boy...

Japanese: わん。
English: Woof.

Japanese: コイツッス。
English: Here he is.

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイッ！
English: Hey, he's cute!

Japanese: なるほどくん、カワイイよ！
English: Look, Nick! Cute dog!

Japanese: （たしかにカワイイが‥‥）
English: (A cute dog...)

Japanese: （コレで何をしろというんだ？）
English: (And this will help us... how?)

Japanese: わん。
English: Woof.

Japanese: 次。ヒミツ兵器その２。 ‥‥ツリザオ。
English: Next, Secret Weapon No. 2... A fishing pole!

Japanese: これッス。自分の私物ッス。
English: Here, this is my own personal pole.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 ‥‥相手はカイジュウですよ。
English: Detective Gumshoe... we're looking for a monster...

Japanese: そッスよ。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: 釣れるワケないじゃないですか！
English: How are we supposed to catch a whole sea monster with a fishing pole!?

Japanese: そんなの、やってみなきゃ わからねッス！
English: Never know 'til you try, pal!

Japanese: えー、次が最後ッス。
English: Okay, this next one is the last one.

Japanese: （‥‥もう最後かよ）
English: (No, please, I'm already overwhelmed by our choices.)

Japanese: ヒミツ兵器その３。 ‥‥金属探知機。
English: Secret Weapon No. 3! A metal detector!

Japanese: これッス。
English: Here.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 ‥‥相手はナマモノですよ。
English: Detective Gumshoe... we're looking for something alive.

Japanese: そッスよ。
English: Right!

Japanese: よりによって、どうして 金属探知機なんですか！
English: How are we supposed to find it with a metal detector!?

Japanese: 空きカンとか食べてるかも 知れないッス！
English: Hey, you never know! It might have been eating soda cans!

Japanese: さぁ！　どれにするッス？
English: Well, which will it be!?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 迷っちゃうねえ、なるほどくん。
English: I can't make up my mind, Nick. They all seem so perfect!

Japanese: たしかに、ものスゴく迷わされる ラインナップだな‥‥。
English: I can't make up my mind, either... for the totally opposite reason.

Japanese: （しかたない、とりあえず 借りてみるか‥‥）
English: (Oh well, I suppose it can't hurt to borrow one of them.)

Japanese: イトノコさん。 他の道具、貸してくれますか？
English: Detective Gumshoe, can we borrow one of the other things?

Japanese: おっ！　いいッスよ。
English: Hmm? Yeah, sure, pal!

Japanese: でも、さっき貸したヤツは 返してもらうッスからね。
English: But I have to take back the last one I lent you, department policy.

Japanese: じゃ、選ぶッス。 ‥‥どれにするッスか？
English: Well, take your pick. Which will it be?

Japanese: じゃあその、ミサイルを 貸してください。
English: Can we borrow Missile?

Japanese: わかったッス。 ‥‥かわいがってほしいッス。
English: Sure thing, pal. Be good to 'im!

Japanese: わん。
English: Woof.

Japanese: きゃあきゃあ。かーわいい！
English: He's sooooo cute!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh boy...)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事から、 警察犬《ミサイル》を借りた。
English: Borrowed K-9 unit Missile from Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: じゃあその、ボロっちい ツリザオを借ります。
English: Can we borrow that flimsy- looking fishing pole?

Japanese: わかったッス。
English: Sure thing, pal.

Japanese: あ、もし折れたら、ちゃんと ゴミ捨て場に捨てるッスよ。
English: Oh, if it breaks, be sure to dispose of it properly, okay?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Er... right.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事から、 私物の《ツリザオ》を借りた。
English: Borrowed Fishing Pole from Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: じゃあその、金属探知機 おねがいできますか。
English: Can we borrow that metal detector?

Japanese: わかったッス。
English: Sure thing, pal.

Japanese: こんなシロモノで、 何を探すのやら‥‥。
English: I'm not sure what we're going to find with this...

Japanese: 相手はカイジュウッス。 ジョーシキは捨てるッス。
English: Remember: you're hunting for a monster. Anything is possible! Anything!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事から、 《金属探知機》を借りた。
English: Borrowed Metal Detector from Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ミサイルは、刑事課でも 大人気ッス。
English: Missile is a big star in Criminal Affairs.

Japanese: でも、この子、なんで “ミサイル”なんですか？
English: Why's he named "Missile"?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。自分は 知らないッスけど‥‥。
English: Huh... now that you mention it, I'm not sure.

Japanese: コイツにはピッタリな 名前だって、よく聞くッスよ。
English: But I hear he's aptly-named, for what it's worth.

Japanese: なんか、ある日突然、 ツリザオがほしくなったッス。
English: It's funny, one day I woke up and I wanted a fishing pole.

Japanese: 釣りが好きなんですか？
English: Do you like fishing?

Japanese: やったことないッス。
English: I've never gone.

Japanese: とにかく、ムショーにツリザオが ほしくて、買ったッス。
English: I just wanted one so bad, I went out and bought it.

Japanese: 使ったコト、ないッス。
English: I've never used it, actually.

Japanese: （ケイジってシゴトも、 大変なんだなあ‥‥）
English: (Must have been one of those mid-life crisis things...)

Japanese: 見てのとおり、金属探知機ッス。
English: As you can see, it's a metal detector.

Japanese: 土の中に埋もれた弾丸なんか 探したりするッス。
English: We use it to look for bullets in the ground.

Japanese: それでカイジュウが見つけられたら ハクがつくッス。
English: If you can find that monster with it, all the better!

Japanese: （見つかるかよ！）
English: (I'm not so hopeful...)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 26 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 イトノコ刑事、いないぞ‥‥。
English: Hmm... Detective Gumshoe isn't here...

Japanese: そういえば“捜査会議がある” って言ってたよね。
English: Now that you mention it, didn't he say he had a meeting to go to?

Japanese: ああ、そうか。 ‥‥また、後で来ようか。
English: Ah, that's right... Let's come back later.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 26 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: おっ！　ひさしぶりッス！
English: Hey, pal! Long time no see!

Japanese: まァた、サエないカオ してるッスな‥‥。
English: You don't look so happy.

Japanese: 今度はどうしたッス？
English: What's wrong this time?

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、聞きたいことが あって来たんですよ。
English: Actually, we wanted to ask you something...

Japanese: 聞きたいこと‥‥？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: わかったッス。
English: I get ya.

Japanese: どうやら、アンタにはＤＬ６号事件 の資料が必要みたいッス。
English: Sounds like you need information on the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: この先に、署の資料室があるッス。
English: Through there is the Station's Records Room.

Japanese: トクベツに入室を許可するッス。 調べてみるといいッス。
English: I'll give you special permission to go in and find what you need.

Japanese: さすが、イトノコ刑事さん！
English: All right! Way to go, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: ささ。なるほどくん。 行ってみようよ！　資料室！
English: Okay, Nick! To the Records Room!

Japanese: （御剣の過去と向き合う ときが来たか‥‥）
English: (I guess it's time we faced Edgeworth's past...)

Japanese: ひょうたん湖公園に、 貸しボート屋がありますよね？
English: You know the boat rental shop down at Gourd Lake?

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Oh, yeah...

Japanese: 明日は、そこのオジさんが 証人で出てくるんですよね？
English: The old man who runs it is appearing as a witness in court tomorrow, right?

Japanese: ‥‥まいったッスね。 ゴクヒだったッスけど‥‥。
English: Huh...!? How'd you-- Hmm. That was supposed to be top secret.

Japanese: 刑事さん。‥‥あのヒト、 いったいダレなんですか？
English: Do you know who that old man is, Detective?

Japanese: それが‥‥わからねッス。
English: Actually... I don't.

Japanese: ちょっと変わった人物で、 話が通じないッス。
English: He's a bit of an odd bird... I haven't been able to get a straight answer out of him.

Japanese: 証人としての説得力が 足りないと判断したから、
English: I decided first that he wasn't persuasive enough to stand and testify as a witness.

Japanese: カメラマンの大沢木　ナツミを 証人席に呼んだッス。
English: That's why we called Ms. Lotta Hart yesterday.

Japanese: 彼がいったい、ナニモノか‥‥？
English: As for who he is...

Japanese: 素性・その他、いっさい ナゾッス。
English: We have absolutely no idea.

Japanese: ナゾ‥‥。 アヤシイなあ‥‥。
English: Hmm... sounds suspicious!
Hmm...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 おねがいがあります。
English: Detective Gumshoe, please, help us...

Japanese: な‥‥なんスか？
English: H-huh?

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件”の詳細を 教えてください。
English: We need to know about the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: 御剣の父親が亡くなった事件。
English: That was when Edgeworth's father died.

Japanese: どうも、それが今回の事件にも カンケイがあるみたいなんです。
English: I can't help but think that it has something to do with this current case.

Japanese: 実は、自分もくわしいことは 知らないッス。
English: To tell the truth, I don't know much about DL-6 either.

Japanese: 御剣検事から、資料を読むことも 禁じられているッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth forbade us from reading the file.

Japanese: だから、アンタたちにも、 資料を見せるわけにはいかねッス。
English: So... I'm afraid I can't show them to you, either, pal.

Japanese: ええーっ。
English: What!?

Japanese: ただし。
English: However...

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件が、今回の事件に カンケイがあると、
English: If you can convince me somehow that the DL-6 Incident is related to this case...

Japanese: 自分をナットクさせてくれたら、 考えなおしてもいいッス。
English: Well, I guess I'd consider opening the file up.

Japanese: イトノコさん。 コレ、役に立ちましたよ。
English: Thanks, Detective Gumshoe. This was really helpful.

Japanese: そ‥‥そッスか。
English: R-really?

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、 その意外そうなカオは。
English: You seem surprised!

Japanese: い、いや。‥‥ベツに。 よかったッスな。
English: Huh? N-no, not at all. Glad it worked out for you.

Japanese: なんスか。‥‥オウム？
English: What's that? A parrot?

Japanese: ボート屋のオジさんのオウムです。
English: The old man at the boat rental shop's parrot.

Japanese: このオウム、あの事件のことを 知っていたんですよ。
English: The parrot knew about that "incident"...

Japanese: あの事件？
English: That incident?

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件”ですよ、 モチロン。
English: DL-6.

Japanese: な、なんスとォ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: サユリさんサユリさん。 何かわすれたこと、ないかな？
English: Polly! Polly! Have we forgotten something?
*squawk*

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件を忘れるな”
English: "Don't forget DL-6!" *squawk*
H-huh!?

Japanese: たぶん、あのオジさんが教え込んだ コトバなんでしょう。
English: I'm pretty sure that old man must have taught her that word...

Japanese: しかし、なぜあのオジさんが、 ＤＬ６号事件を‥‥？
English: Yeah, but how would that old man know about the DL-6 Incident?

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥。
English: Wait! What if...

Japanese: あのオジさん、ＤＬ６号事件の 関係者なのでは‥‥？
English: What if that old man was connected to DL-6?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥！
English: N-Nick! You think he might be!?

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 26 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: 今日はセンセイ、休みを とっているそうだ。
English: Apparently, Mr. Grossberg is on vacation today.

Japanese: 何か用があったら、 明日、また来よう‥‥。
English: Well, I guess I can come back tomorrow if I need anything.

Japanese: 星影センセイの絵、 どうやら帰ってこないみたいだな。
English: I guess Mr. Grossberg isn't getting that painting back.

Japanese: ちょっとかわいそうな 気もする‥‥。
English: I feel kind of bad for him...

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... They don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 管理人の小屋
English: December 26 Caretaker's Shack

Japanese: ‥‥メグミ！
English: Meg! That you!?

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: それに、ユキヒロじゃないか！
English: Hey, is that Keith with you!?

Japanese: お前たち、今までどこに‥‥！ シンパイかけおって‥‥。
English: Where have you two been! I've been worried sick.

Japanese: な、なるほどくん。 ‥‥ささ、どうぞ。
English: N-Nick... you handle this.

Japanese: いやいや。 ここは真宵ちゃんが‥‥。
English: Uh, I think I'll leave this one up to you, Maya.

Japanese: メグミやッ！
English: Meg!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!?

Japanese: やっと決心してくれたかい。
English: Finally made up your mind, have you?

Japanese: は‥‥はい？
English: M-my mind?

Japanese: このソバ屋、ついでくれるか！
English: You'll run the pasta shop when I'm gone?

Japanese: そ‥‥そばや？
English: P-pasta?

Japanese: そーかそーか。
English: Glad to hear it, glad to hear it!

Japanese: ‥‥とーさん、安心したぞ。
English: You make your old man proud.

Japanese: おマエたち兄妹そろって 出ていっちまったときは、
English: When you kids left the house, I didn't know what to think.

Japanese: どうやってノレンを 守っていこうかと‥‥
English: How'm I supposed to keep this place running, an old man like me?

Japanese: サユリさん。 ‥‥２人がもどってきたよ。
English: Polly! The kids are home!

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ ‥‥い、今の‥‥！
English: N-Nick! What was that!?

Japanese: オウムだよ。 ‥‥止まり木にいるヤツ。
English: A parrot... the one on that perch.

Japanese: ユキヒロや。
English: Keith!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!?

Japanese: “長寿庵”のノレンは オマエにマカせたぞ。
English: I leave the "Wet Noodle" in your capable hands, sonny.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 “長寿庵”ってなに？
English: N-Nick? What's the "Wet Noodle"?

Japanese: 今までの情報からスイリするなら、 たぶんソバ屋の名前だろう。
English: Um, based on the available evidence, I'd say it's the name of his pasta shop.

Japanese: サユリさん。 ‥‥もう安心だなあ。
English: That's a relief, isn't it, Polly?

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: そーかそーか。
English: Ayup...

Japanese: ぐう。
English: *Zzzzz*

Japanese: 寝ちゃった。
English: He fell asleep...

Japanese: 安心したみたいだ。
English: I guess he's relieved.

Japanese: あ。そういえば。
English: You know what? Eh?

Japanese: さっき、タミオくんが来てなあ。
English: Little Terry was just here.

Japanese: “タミオくん”‥‥？
English: "Terry"...?

Japanese: ホレ、オマエが小さかったコロ よく泣かされた、となりの子だよ。
English: Ayup, that kid next door. You always used to make him cry, remember?

Japanese: うすぎたないコートを着た、 ヒゲヅラの‥‥。
English: He was wearin' this tattered old coat. Got himself some whiskers growing out his face.

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事のことか‥‥）
English: (He must be talking about Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: アイツが来てな、 明日、裁判所に来いとさ。
English: He comes up and tells me to come down to court tomorrow.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Really...?

Japanese: （とりあえず、このオジさんから 聞けるのは、こんなとこかな）
English: (Somehow I don't think we're going to get much useful information from this guy.)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。 ‥‥そろそろ帰ろうか。
English: Maya... maybe we should be leaving.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: I think you're right.

Japanese: あ。ちょっと待ってね。 ‥‥最後にもう１つ。
English: Oh, wait--I had one more question.
Huh?

Japanese: サユリさんサユリさん。 何かわすれたこと、ないかな？
English: Polly! Polly! Have we forgotten something?
*squawk*

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件を忘れるな”
English: "Don't forget DL-6!" *squawk*
H-huh!?

Japanese: 今‥‥なんて言った？ なるほどくん。
English: What did she just say, Nick?

Japanese: サユリさん、もう一度！
English: One more time, Polly!

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件を忘れるな”
English: "Don't forget DL-6!" *squawk*

Japanese: （ど‥‥、どういうことだ‥‥！）
English: (What!? The DL-6 Incident?)

Japanese: オジさん！　‥‥いや、 おとーさん‥‥！
English: Hey, mister! I-I mean, Dad!

Japanese: ‥‥ぐう。
English: *Zzzzz*

Japanese: （‥‥このオジさん‥‥）
English: (This is getting weird...)

Japanese: （いったい、ナニモノなんだ？）
English: (Who IS this old guy!?)

Japanese: わあ‥‥。 リッパなオウムだなあ。
English: Wow... what an amazing parrot that is.

Japanese: オハヨー。
English: Good morning!

Japanese: コンニチハ。
English: Hello!

Japanese: ‥‥ムシされた。
English: He ignored me!
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: わすれたのか、メグミ。 まず、名前を呼ばないと。
English: What, you forgot, Meg? You gotta call her name first!

Japanese: なまえ？
English: Her name?

Japanese: サユリさん。 どうだい、最近？
English: Polly! How ya been!?

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: な。
English: See?

Japanese: ‥‥へえ！　そのオウム、 “サユリさん”ていうんだ。
English: Neat! So the parrot's name is "Polly"!

Japanese: 《オウム》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Parrot added to the Court Record.

Japanese: でも、“オハヨー”だけじゃ つまんないね。
English: Too bad all she can say is "hello"...

Japanese: ホッホッホッ！
English: Har har har!

Japanese: サユリさんは、いろんなコトを しゃべるぞお。
English: Old Polly can say lots of things!

Japanese: それには、“合い言葉” が必要なんだなあ。
English: You just need to know the secret words!

Japanese: “合い言葉”‥‥？
English: The "secret words"...?

Japanese: サユリさん、サユリさん。 ‥‥お名前は？
English: Polly! Polly! What's your name?

Japanese: “‥‥サユリサン”
English: "Pol-ly!" *squawk*

Japanese: きゃあ。かわいい！
English: Hee hee! Cute!

Japanese: （‥‥気に入ったみたいだ）
English: (Maya's found a new friend.)

Japanese: あ。かわいい金庫だ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Look, a little safe! Hmm...

Japanese: コラ！　勝手に開けちゃ ダメだよ。
English: Now, don't you go opening people's safes without their permission, Meg!

Japanese: 今は開けられないね。
English: Maybe later...

Japanese: カギがかかってる。
English: It's locked.

Japanese: あ。かわいい金庫だ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Look, a little safe! Hmm...

Japanese: コラ！　勝手に開けちゃ ダメだよ。
English: Now, don't you go opening people's safes without their permission, Meg!

Japanese: 今は開けられないね。
English: Maybe later...

Japanese: カギがかかってる。
English: It's locked.
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: あの‥‥おソバ屋さん、って？
English: Umm... a pasta shop?

Japanese: ジイさんの代から受けついだ “長寿庵”のノレンも、
English: Ayup! To think, the "Wet Noodle," will live on when I'm gone!

Japanese: やっとお前たちに わたすことができるのお。
English: My father started it you know. So that makes you two the third generation!

Japanese: メグミや。
English: Meg...

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!

Japanese: 明日からみっちり、三返りのゴクイ をタタキ込んでやるからなあ。
English: Tomorrow, we'll start with the secrets of dough tossin'!

Japanese: み、みかえり‥‥？
English: D-dough tossing?

Japanese: ユキヒロも。
English: You, too, Keith.

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: オマエも、 リッパな板前になるんだぞ。
English: You'll be the best pasta wrangler the west has ever seen!

Japanese: 板前‥‥って、 おスシ屋さんじゃなかったっけ？
English: P-pasta wrangler? The west? I-isn't pasta from Italy?

Japanese: メグミ
English: Meg!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!

Japanese: 板前といえば、ソバを打つ職人の ことに決まってるだろお？
English: You know the best pasta's always been made west of the Rockies, don't you?

Japanese: そ、そうですよね。 決まってますよね。
English: R-right, of course. Everybody knows that.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...?

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: いつまでつづけるの？ ‥‥この不毛な親子ごっこ。
English: How long do we have to keep up this all-in-the- family charade?

Japanese: （‥このオジさんは、事件のことを 何か知っているハズだ）
English: (This old man must know something about the murder...!)

Japanese: （それを聞き出すまでは、まだ 帰れないぞ‥‥）
English: (We're not leaving until we find out what that is.)
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: あの‥‥。ここ、貸しボート屋 ですよね‥‥？
English: Umm... this is a boat rental shop, right?

Japanese: 何を言っとる！
English: What're you talking about!?

Japanese: ここはソバのしにせ、 “長寿庵”だよ。
English: This here's the palace of pasta, the "Wet Noodle"!

Japanese: なんか知らんが、最近の客は ソバも注文しないで、
English: Though, now that you mention it, we haven't gotten many orders for spaghetti lately.

Japanese: “ボートに乗りたいんですけどぉ” なんて言いやがるからなあ。
English: All the kids come up and say "Yo dude, we wanna ride in one of your boats!"

Japanese: しかたなく、ボートも 置いてあるんだよ。
English: That's why I keep them boats out there.

Japanese: マッタク、ワケわからんなあ。
English: Youngsters these days... Darned if I understand 'em!

Japanese: ホント、ワケわかりませんね。
English: I'm pretty confused myself.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥ダメだよこりゃあ。
English: Nick... This isn't going anywhere.

Japanese: ‥‥でもこのオジさん、 明日の法廷の証人、なんだろ？
English: But this old man is the witness tomorrow, right?

Japanese: 事件について、何か情報を 引き出しておかないと‥‥。
English: We've got to find some way of getting information out of him!
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: いやな。‥‥すっかり わすれっぽくなっちまったから、
English: Ack, my memory's gotten worse of late.

Japanese: ダイジなことはゼンブ、 サユリさんに教えてあるんだよ。
English: That's why I just tell everything important to old Polly here.

Japanese: ダイジなコト？
English: Everything... important?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、たとえば‥‥。
English: Hmm, I wonder...

Japanese: サユリさん。金庫の番号は？
English: Polly! What's the number to the safe!?
*squawk*

Japanese: やったあ！
English: Alright!

Japanese: コ、コラ！　サユリさん！ ‥‥不用心な。
English: H-hey! Polly! Watch it, will ya!

Japanese: フッフッ、なるほどくん。 ‥‥アタマを使わないとね。
English: Heh heh. See, Nick? All it takes is a little clever thinking!

Japanese: （ワルいヤツ‥‥）
English: (And a criminal mind...)

Japanese: ほら、ほら、なるほどくん。 メモメモ。
English: Quick, Nick! Write that number down!

Japanese: い、いいのかな。
English: H-hey! Don't get me involved in your little heist schemes.

Japanese: 時間は忘れたが、ソトは暗かったし たぶん、夜だったと思う。
English: I forget the time, but it was pretty dark outside... probably night, ayup.

Japanese: （夜中の１２時すぎだよ‥‥）
English: (It was after midnight, but okay...)

Japanese: パアン！　て音が聞こえてな。 ソトを見たんじゃ。
English: Then I heard this "bang!" So I looked outside.

Japanese: するともう１発、パアンと。
English: Then I heard another one. "Bang!"

Japanese: しばらくすると、 ボートが帰ってきてなあ。
English: A little while later, this boat comes back.

Japanese: 若い男が、窓の外を 通りすぎて行ったよ。
English: Then a young man walked by my window here.

Japanese: ヒトコト、つぶやいてな。
English: He was mutterin' something to himself, ayup.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんて‥‥？
English: What did he say?

Japanese: あれは、そう‥‥
English: Ayup...

Japanese: わすれた。
English: I forgot.

Japanese: 明日の法廷までには 思い出しておくよ。
English: I'll remember tomorrow by court time, promise.

Japanese: それじゃ、おそいよ‥‥。
English: We need to know earlier than that...!
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: そんなモンより、ユキヒロ。
English: Now, listen here, Keith.

Japanese: ２人で約束した、究極の変わりソバ を早くカンセイさせようなあ。
English: Remember that tricolor pasta we were talking about?

Japanese: “レインボーソバ”の最後の１色、 インディゴ・ブルーのソバを！
English: Our "Rainbolioli"! I figured out the last color we should use! Indigo Blue!

Japanese: いんでぃご・ぶるー？
English: Indigo... Blue?

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥。 他のものをつきつけてみるか）
English: (That didn't seem to work the way I thought it might.)
*Zzzzz*

Japanese: 弁護士バッジ‥‥？
English: That a lawyer's badge?

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes, it is!

Japanese: （バッジだけ見て 弁護士だってわかるなんて‥‥）
English: (I don't believe it!)

Japanese: （このオジさんが初めてだぞ‥‥）
English: (This old guy is the first person to recognize my badge!)

Japanese: ‥‥ワカったぞ‥‥。
English: I get it.
Huh?

Japanese: さてはオマエ‥‥
English: Ayup... I got you figured out now!

Japanese: ユキヒロじゃないなあッ！
English: You're not Keith!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick!

Japanese: とりあえず、 ゴカイを解くチャンスだよ！
English: Now's our chance to clear things up!

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん。
English: Umm... sir.

Japanese: ぼくは、ユキヒロ じゃありません。
English: No, I'm not Keith.

Japanese: ちなみに、あたしも メグミちゃんじゃありませんから！
